---
title: "AI for Coding"
sidebar: false
---

## Course Overview

This course aims to equip economists who regularly use Stata and other modern
statistical programming languages to leverage AI-assisted coding tools
effectively and responsibly. It introduces participants to AI coding assistants
(GitHub Copilot) within the Positron IDE, across two 3-hour sessions with a
self-study period in between.

## Syllabus

### Prerequisites

Complete setup steps **before the first session**: install Stata 19+, R, Python, Positron, Quarto, and Git; configure GitHub and Copilot access; install required extensions.

### Day 1 — Introduction to AI-Assisted Coding (3 hours)

- Software overview: Positron, GitHub Copilot, and Stata setup
- Live demo: a typical data workflow using AI
- AI overview: how LLMs work, key concepts, strengths and limitations
- Responsible use: securing sensitive data and applying guardrails
- Self-study exercises overview and Q&A

### Self-Study (2–3 days)

Independent practice with structured case studies: reproduce old code, reproduce from an example, or try a new language.

### Day 2 — Everyday AI-Assisted Coding (3 hours)

- Q&A from self-study: challenges and discoveries
- Planning and executing complex tasks with parallel agentic workflows
- Context engineering: `#tools`, `skills`, `MCP`, `/prompts`, `/agents`
- Hands-on exercises integrating AI tools into real workflows
- Discussion, feedback, and next steps

See the Materials page for full session details, slides, and exercises.

## Schedule & Plan

### Morning Course — In Person and Virtual [Join Teams Channel](https://teams.microsoft.com/meet/27612063989198?p=jK5O2M12JmSoQGPFcg)

| Session | Date | Time (ET) |
|---------|------|-----------|
| 1 | Mon Apr 20 | 9:00 AM–12:00 PM |
| 2 | Thu Apr 23 | 9:00 AM–12:00 PM |

### Evening Course (Repeat) — Virtual [Join Teams Channel](https://teams.microsoft.com/meet/22792960965888?p=7H3zxnQKgcG0elimZb)

| Session | Date | Time (ET) | Time Jakarta (GMT+7) |
|---------|------|-----------|---------|
| 1 | Mon Apr 20 | 9:00 PM–12:00 AM | Tue Apr 21, 9:00 AM–12:00 PM |
| 2 | Thu Apr 23 | 9:00 PM–12:00 AM | Fri Apr 24, 9:00 AM–12:00 PM |

## Learning Objectives

By the end of this course, participants will be able to:

**Understand**

- Explain core LLM concepts (tokens, prompts, context windows, agents) and how they affect AI-assisted coding
- Recognize the strengths and limitations of AI code assistants for analytical work

**Apply**

- Use an AI assistant to read, refactor, and revise existing code
- Structure prompts and context to guide AI output for multi-step analytical tasks
- Supervise and course-correct AI-generated code through iterative review

**Govern**

- Apply data-security practices when using AI tools with sensitive or confidential data
- Follow responsible-use guidelines for AI-assisted research workflows

## Logistics & Organisation

*Placeholder – logistics, access, communication channels, etc.*
